title: vigna unguiculata subsp. mensensis
intro: Vigna unguiculata subsp. mensensis, also known as the climbing cowpea, is a subspecies of the Vigna unguiculata plant, a legume native to Africa. The climbing cowpea has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. The herbal preparations of Vigna unguiculata subsp. mensensis typically involve decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
